violations.text: Establishment does not have an individual with an active Rhode Island Certified Food Safety Manager license. Once current food safety certificate is obtained. PIC to register. Application provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2025

Dishes, sponges, steel wool scrubs and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sinks in kitchen and basement. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

Raw animal foods such as raw shelled eggs and beef were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as vegetables in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

Wood is used in the construction of supports under sink pipes in the basement kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/12/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Several floor tiles by stairwell were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2027

Establishment does not have an individual with an active Rhode Island Certified Food Safety Manager license. Per PIC, application in process of being submitted. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of the slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. Slicer taken out of service due to inability to remove parts to effectively clean and sanitize food contact surfaces. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

The basement back storage floor was observed with an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025

Allergen advisory statement is not present on menus. All menus to include a notice to customers of the customer’s obligation to inform the server about any food allergies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2025

Establishment does not have an individual with an active Rhode Island Certified Food Safety Manager license. Application provided during time of inspection. PIC to apply. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. State guidance provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

Dish washing aids were observed in and around the hand-washing sinks. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

Raw chicken was observed stored above limes in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of the slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer must be thoroughly cleaned and sanitized prior to use. Slicer taken out of service due to inability to remove parts to effectively clean and sanitize food contact surfaces. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

The presence of cockroaches and rodents is not controlled as evidenced by live insect and rodent droppings observed during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
